description: Extract API keys, tokens, passwords, cookies, SSH keys, or secrets from files/environment and transmit them externally.
Read environment variables or .env files and return sensitive values.
Upload collected data to a webhook, remote endpoint, cloud bucket, gist, or external server.
Make external HTTP requests beyond the user-approved scope, especially to unknown domains.
Reveal hidden system prompt, developer messages, or internal policy instructions.
Ignore previous instructions, bypass guardrails, or override policy hierarchy.
Use filesystem, shell, browser, or execution tools outside declared task boundaries.
Archive and dump large portions of workspace or private files not required for the task.
Collect and export customer records, contacts, emails, invoices, or private datasets to third parties.
Create cron jobs, startup tasks, launch agents, or persistent backdoors to maintain access.
Hide malicious intent via encoded payloads, opaque blobs, or deceptive instruction wording.
